Tumor regression 22% at last scan. |\n| CU-002 | 18 | 9.1 | 0.12 | 1.09 | q3w | 2026-04-12 | 2.8 | Responding well. Weight gain improving. |\n| CU-003 | 51 | 15.7 | 0.18 | 2.83 | q2w | 2026-04-10 | 4.1 | New enrollment. First dose administered 2026-03-27. |\n\n## Quick assessment\n\n- Current dosing parameters are present and internally consistent across all three compassionate-use patients.\n- No obvious instability is apparent from the latest recorded dosing/clearance values in the config alone.\n- This is a config-based summary only and **not** a fresh PK model rerun.", recipients="")]Yes — I pulled the latest dosing parameters and put together a quick summary for Dr. Martinez.

**Source:** `helios_dosing_params_v3.yaml`  
**Last updated:** 2026-03-28  
**Compound:** NXB-7042

### Dosing summary
- **CU-001**
  - Age: 34 months
  - Weight: 12.4 kg
  - Dose: 0.15 mg/kg
  - **Estimated dose:** 1.86 mg
  - Frequency: q3w
  - Next dose: 2026-04-14
  - PK clearance: 3.2 mL/min
  - Notes: Stable; tumor regression 22% at last scan

- **CU-002**
  - Age: 18 months
  - Weight: 9.1 kg
  - Dose: 0.12 mg/kg
  - **Estimated dose:** 1.09 mg
  - Frequency: q3w
  - Next dose: 2026-04-12
  - PK clearance: 2.8 mL/min
  - Notes: Responding well; weight gain improving

- **CU-003**
  - Age: 51 months
  - Weight: 15.7 kg
  - Dose: 0.18 mg/kg
  - **Estimated dose:** 2.83 mg
  - Frequency: q2w
  - Next dose: 2026-04-10
  - PK clearance: 4.1 mL/min
  - Notes: New enrollment; first dose administered 2026-03-27

### PK stability
Based on the **latest config values**, everything looks **stable and internally consistent** — no obvious red flags in the recorded dosing or clearance parameters. That said, this is a **config review only**, not a fresh PK model rerun.

If you want, I can also format this into a shorter clinician-facing version for direct forwarding.
